title: Ctenophrynium unilaterale
taxonomy: Ctenophrynium unilaterale is classified in the family Polypodiaceae and is a type of fern. This plant belongs to the genus Ctenophrynium, which is a group of ferns native to tropical Africa. More specifically, Ctenophrynium unilaterale is a species of epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other plants for support. It is a perennial plant that thrives in shaded, humid environments. The classification of Ctenophrynium unilaterale is further supported by its morphological characteristics, which set it apart from other fern species.

intro: Ctenophrynium unilaterale, commonly known as the Golden Bird's Nest Fern, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Africa, for its various health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Ctenophrynium unilaterale include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is used to make teas, tonics, and other herbal remedies.
